project.name: Rental Home
project.description: Full-stack rental property management application with React frontend, Supabase backend, governed delivery via GWC, and tenant self-service surfaces including Telegram Mini App.
limitations: Local checkout analysis at one point in time; working tree assumed clean.
Production database state and Supabase RLS policies not inspected at runtime.
GWC submodule state not fully validated.

nodes.name: Repository governance entry
nodes.summary: Defines GWC-first execution, evidence provenance, boot order, intake card, gate lifecycle, and approval commands for Rental Home.

nodes.name: Skill-routing README
nodes.summary: Documents the repository's AI skill routing and onboarding guidance for delivery agents.

nodes.name: Task control board
nodes.summary: Tracks spec-backed delivery, active work, completed gates, and priority queue for Rental Home.

nodes.name: Codebase graph source of truth
nodes.summary: Canonical guide for layered architecture, requirements traceability, and codebase map for Rental Home.

nodes.name: Semantic architecture baseline report
nodes.summary: Published versioned semantic architecture baseline with 33 nodes, 54 edges, priority findings, and guided tour.

nodes.name: Application package contract
nodes.summary: Defines the React/Vite/TypeScript stack, pnpm workspace, scripts for dev, build, test, migration, and delivery.

nodes.name: TypeScript compiler configuration
nodes.summary: Configures strict TypeScript compilation with path aliases for the Vite application.

nodes.name: Vite build configuration
nodes.summary: Configures React plugin, path aliases, dev server, and build output for the Vite application.

nodes.name: Build, test, and database delivery pipeline
nodes.summary: Runs frozen dependency installation, typecheck, build, unit tests, Playwright E2E tests, and conditional Supabase migration delivery.

nodes.name: GWC governance submodule
nodes.summary: Pinned governance package used by Rental Home for gate lifecycle, runbooks, agent instructions, and project profiles.

nodes.name: Application router and route guards
nodes.summary: Composes admin, tenant, public-room, Telegram, and UA-graph routes with ProtectedRoute, AdminRoute, and TenantAccessGate guards.

nodes.name: Dependency composition root
nodes.summary: Selects mock or Supabase implementations, exports repository singletons for homeRental, finance, auth, tenant, admin, notifications, and services.

nodes.name: Shared domain model catalog
nodes.summary: Defines homes, rooms, tenants, contracts, services, invoices, payments, expenses, meter readings, maintenance, and finance ledger models.

nodes.name: Maintenance domain types
nodes.summary: Defines MaintenanceQueueQuery, CreateMaintenanceRequestCommand, TriageMaintenanceRequestCommand, TransitionMaintenanceRequestCommand.

nodes.name: Billing readiness domain types
nodes.summary: Defines MonthlyBillingGenerationInput and MonthlyBillingGenerationResult for billing preparation.

nodes.name: Batch invoice payment domain types
nodes.summary: Defines PreviewRoomBillsInput, PreviewRoomBillsResult, MarkRoomBillsPaidBatchInput, BatchMarkPaidResult.

nodes.name: Room public listing domain types
nodes.summary: Defines PublicRoomView and UpdateRoomPublicListingInput for public-facing room projections.

nodes.name: HomeRental repository port
nodes.summary: A broad repository interface spanning dashboard, homes, rooms, tenants, contracts, services, invoices, payments, expenses, maintenance, meter readings, and public listings.

nodes.name: Operations repository port
nodes.summary: Defines operations-specific data access interface for operational workflows.

nodes.name: Auth repository port
nodes.summary: Defines authentication interface for admin user sessions.

nodes.name: Tenant auth repository port
nodes.summary: Defines tenant-specific authentication and authorization interface.

nodes.name: Tenant access repository port
nodes.summary: Defines tenant access control, app access status, and tenant account management interface.

nodes.name: Finance report repository port
nodes.summary: Defines finance reporting interface for cashflow, ledger, and debt aging.

nodes.name: Financial reconciliation repository port
nodes.summary: Defines financial reconciliation interface for matching payments and invoices.

nodes.name: Service catalog repository port
nodes.summary: Defines CRUD interface for service catalog management.

nodes.name: Notification delivery repository port
nodes.summary: Defines notification delivery interface for WhatsApp and other channels.

nodes.name: Admin home repository port
nodes.summary: Defines admin-specific home management interface.

nodes.name: Admin user repository port
nodes.summary: Defines admin user management interface for user CRUD and role assignment.

nodes.name: Tenant Telegram link repository port
nodes.summary: Defines Telegram linking interface for tenant identity binding.

nodes.name: Active-home repository decorator
nodes.summary: Uses a Proxy and method-handler map to filter home-scoped data, with explicit policy methods for active-home behavior.

nodes.name: Supabase lifecycle adapter
nodes.summary: Maps contracts and service assignments, derives display status, and implements narrow schema compatibility fallback for legacy projections.

nodes.name: Supabase client singleton
nodes.summary: Creates and exports the Supabase client instance configured from environment variables.

nodes.name: Supabase auth adapter
nodes.summary: Implements AuthRepository using Supabase Auth for admin session and user management.

nodes.name: Supabase operations adapter
nodes.summary: Implements OperationsRepository for operational data access via Supabase.

nodes.name: Supabase finance report adapter
nodes.summary: Implements FinanceReportRepository for cashflow, ledger, and debt aging via Supabase queries.

nodes.name: Supabase tenant adapter
nodes.summary: Implements tenant-specific data access for the Supabase backend.

nodes.name: Supabase tenant access adapter
nodes.summary: Implements tenant access control, app status management, and identity verification via Supabase RPCs.

nodes.name: Supabase tenant auth adapter
nodes.summary: Implements TenantAuthRepository for tenant login, session, and callback handling.

nodes.name: Supabase notification delivery adapter
nodes.summary: Implements notification delivery via Supabase for WhatsApp and other notification channels.

nodes.name: UA graph visualization page
nodes.summary: Public React route at /architecture/ua-graph that fetches and renders the semantic knowledge graph as an interactive SVG with search, layer grouping, and node inspection.

nodes.name: UA graph TypeScript types
nodes.summary: Defines UAGraph, UAGraphNode, UAGraphEdge, UAGraphLayer, UAGraphTourStep, and UAGraphProject interfaces for the knowledge graph data structure.

nodes.name: UA graph page styles
nodes.summary: CSS styles for the UA graph visualization page including canvas, detail panel, layers, and responsive layout.

nodes.name: Authentication debug panel
nodes.summary: Reads the Supabase client directly to expose auth state, user profile, and RLS diagnostics during development.

nodes.name: Environment mode detection
nodes.summary: Exports isMockMode flag to switch between mock and Supabase adapter implementations.

nodes.name: Database migration catalog
nodes.summary: Collection of 60+ Supabase migration files covering schema setup, RLS policies, RPC functions, views, tenant access, notifications, and finance truth tables.

nodes.name: Landlord and admin web application
nodes.summary: Protected operational interface for rooms, tenants, contracts, invoices, expenses, services, meter readings, reports, maintenance, operations, and admin user/home management.

nodes.name: Tenant self-service web application
nodes.summary: Tenant-gated interface for invoices, payments, meter submissions, maintenance requests, and profile management.

nodes.name: Public room listing projection
nodes.summary: Unauthenticated route that exposes only a deliberate subset of room data for public viewing.

nodes.name: Public UA graph visualization
nodes.summary: Unauthenticated route at /architecture/ua-graph rendering the semantic knowledge graph as an interactive SVG dashboard for architecture exploration.

nodes.name: Telegram Mini App surface
nodes.summary: Alternative tenant-facing shell integrated in Telegram for invoice viewing and basic tenant operations.

nodes.name: Supabase backend
nodes.summary: Provides Postgres, Auth, RLS, RPC functions, Storage, and real-time subscriptions as the canonical backend authority boundary.

nodes.name: Vercel frontend hosting
nodes.summary: Builds and deploys the browser application from the GitHub CI pipeline.

nodes.name: Property operations
nodes.summary: Homes, rooms, services, occupancy, meter readings, and room public listing.

nodes.name: Tenancy and leasing
nodes.summary: Tenant identity, contract lifecycle (create, move-in, notice, move-out, renew, terminate), and notice period management.

nodes.name: Billing and finance
nodes.summary: Invoice generation, trusted payments, expense tracking, finance reconciliation, monthly cashflow, debt aging, and batch bill payment.

nodes.name: Maintenance workflow
nodes.summary: Tenant-safe request intake, admin triage, controlled status transitions (open, in-progress, done, cancelled), and priority management.

nodes.name: Tenant access and identity
nodes.summary: Tenant accounts, app access gating, Telegram linking, auth callback, and review workflow.

nodes.name: Global service-locator coupling
nodes.summary: Feature modules import repository singletons from dependencies.ts rather than receiving them through injection at feature boundaries.

nodes.name: Oversized repository port
nodes.summary: HomeRentalRepository combines unrelated bounded contexts (property, tenancy, billing, maintenance, payments, public listing) into one interface.

nodes.name: Proxy policy bypass risk
nodes.summary: Only methods explicitly overridden in the Proxy policy map are scoped; omitted methods pass through unmodified to the underlying repository.

nodes.name: Application and schema deployment skew
nodes.summary: Frontend deployment and Supabase migration delivery are independently gated, creating potential schema compatibility gaps.

nodes.name: Documentation and runtime drift
nodes.summary: Hand-maintained architecture and route documentation can drift from package versions and actual router definitions.

nodes.name: Product and agent-platform dual center
nodes.summary: The repository serves both as the Rental Home product codebase and as an AI agent platform with GWC governance, Kiro specs, and skill routing.

nodes.name: Debug feature boundary exception
nodes.summary: AuthDebugPanel imports the Supabase adapter directly and can expose auth/database diagnostics if included in an unsafe surface.

nodes.name: Growing port interface surface
nodes.summary: Dependencies.ts now exports 12+ repository interfaces; port surface area is expanding as tenant access, notifications, Telegram, and admin management domains are added.

nodes.name: Narrow schema compatibility fallback
nodes.summary: Contract reads retry a legacy projection only for specific known schema-lag scenarios rather than using catch-all error suppression.

nodes.name: Spec-driven delivery traceability
nodes.summary: Kiro specs, task state, PR evidence, CI, and GWC gates create end-to-end traceability from requirements to production.

nodes.name: Public architecture evidence surface
nodes.summary: PUB-UA-01 adds a read-only public route serving the semantic architecture graph, making architecture evidence accessible without authentication.

nodes.name: Mock and Supabase adapter pairs
nodes.summary: Every repository has a mock and Supabase implementation pair, switchable via isMockMode, enabling development without backend.

nodes.name: Multi-surface tenant access gating
nodes.summary: Tenants access web and Telegram surfaces through TenantAccessGate with app status, auth callback, and Telegram link identity binding.

layers.name: Governance and Knowledge
layers.description: Repository rules, architectural documentation, task truth, and GWC governance submodule.

layers.name: Delivery Surfaces
layers.description: Browser-facing admin, tenant, public-room, public UA-graph, and Telegram experiences.

layers.name: Application Composition
layers.description: Routing, guards, dependency selection, and global repository singletons.

layers.name: Domain and Contracts
layers.description: Business entities, bounded contexts, repository interfaces, and specialized domain types.

layers.name: Adapters and Data
layers.description: Active-home policy, Supabase adapters, database migrations, and backend services.

layers.name: UA Graph Feature
layers.description: Public architecture graph visualization added in PUB-UA-01, routes, types, and styles.

layers.name: Quality and Delivery
layers.description: Package configuration, CI pipeline, frontend hosting, and database migration delivery.

tour.title: Start with authority and repository truth
tour.description: Read AGENTS.md, the GWC gitlink, TASK.md, and both codebase and semantic architecture docs before interpretation.

tour.title: Understand the five user surfaces
tour.description: Follow router.tsx through admin, tenant, public-room, public UA-graph, and Telegram routes (updated from the original 4 surfaces).

tour.title: Inspect dependency composition
tour.description: Review how app/dependencies.ts selects mock versus Supabase implementations across 12+ repository ports.

tour.title: Map the business core
tour.description: Read the shared domain model catalog and then separate property, tenancy, billing, maintenance, and tenant-access bounded contexts.

tour.title: Find the main architectural pressure points
tour.description: HomeRentalRepository spans most business contexts, driving broad adapter surface and proxy bypass risk.

tour.title: Trace schema compatibility and new feature patterns
tour.description: The lifecycle adapter uses a narrow legacy projection fallback. The new public UA-graph feature follows a dependency-free pattern without importing the service locator.

tour.title: Review delivery, verification boundaries, and new surfaces
tour.description: CI validates typecheck, build, unit tests, and E2E tests. Database migrations are conditionally delivered. The public UA graph is served as a static JSON asset.
